title: Reading and Use of English – Part 8
description: You are going to read an article about four people who took up a musical instrument as adults. For questions 47–56, choose from the people A–D. The people may be chosen more than once.

content.title: The Late Bloomers: Adult Music Learners

texts.B:
### Marco, The Cello
'I wanted a sound that resonated with my soul,' Marco says. [51]The most frustrating part was the physical discomfort in my neck and shoulders, which I hadn't anticipated.[/51] [48]I was surprised to find that my hand-eye coordination improved drastically, which helped me in my hobby of amateur carpentry.[/48] [53]I learned that music isn't about perfection; it's about the courage to be vulnerable and make mistakes.[/53] [50]I realized that my background as a math teacher helped me understand the complex time signatures much faster than others.[/50] [47]Initially, the sheer discipline required for daily practice was a shock to my routine.[/47] It was an arduous but rewarding path.
texts.A:
### Sarah, The Jazz Piano
Sarah started piano after a long hiatus. 'I had to unlearn my classical training,' she explains. [47]I hadn't expected the mental fatigue of improvisation to be so draining in the first few weeks.[/47] [54]What kept me going was the local jazz community, which was incredibly welcoming to a novice like me.[/54] [52]I hadn't anticipated the emotional release of finally being able to express my own melodies rather than reading notes.[/52] [49]The experience taught me that creativity is a muscle that needs constant, gentle exercise.[/49] [56]If my husband hadn't bought me a keyboard for my birthday, I’d still be just thinking about it.[/56] It was a liberating shift.
texts.C:
### Victor, The Bagpipes
Victor chose an instrument that turned heads. 'It’s a physical workout,' he notes. [51]I struggled with the lung capacity required, which was much more demanding than I’d ever imagined.[/51] [48]I noticed that my general lung health and breath control improved, which was a nice side effect.[/48] [53]I realized that learning an instrument is a form of meditation that requires absolute presence.[/53] [50]I found that the discipline I used in my previous career as a pilot was perfect for the rigorous practice schedule.[/50] [55]It took a while to adjust to the attention I received when practicing, which felt quite intrusive at first.[/55] [52]I’d initially worried I was too old, but the music didn't care about my age.
texts.D:
### Elena, The Electric Guitar
'I wanted to rediscover my rebellious side,' Elena laughs. [49]The project made me realize that it’s never too late to reinvent yourself or try something entirely outside your comfort zone.[/49] [56]I wouldn't have found the teacher if I hadn't stumbled upon a viral video of an 80-year-old rock star.[/56] [54]I was moved by the camaraderie in my adult 'band camp', where we all shared our beginner struggles.[/54] [47]I hadn't expected the physical pain in my fingertips to be so distracting during the first month.[/47] [55]I found the initial technical jargon of pedals and amps incredibly confusing and frustrating.[/55] [52]I’d been concerned about the noise, but modern headphones solved that problem immediately.

questions.question: mentions being surprised by the mental or physical toll of the initial learning phase?
questions.explanation: Sarah (B) mentions mental fatigue from improvisation, and Elena (D) mentions the pain in her fingertips.

questions.question: notes a positive physical improvement that resulted from practicing their instrument?
questions.explanation: Marco (A) improved his hand-eye coordination, and Victor (C) improved his lung health.

questions.question: expresses a shift in their understanding of creativity or personal growth?
questions.explanation: Sarah (B) learned creativity is a muscle, and Elena (D) realized it's never too late to reinvent oneself.

questions.question: mentions how their professional background or discipline was advantageous?
questions.explanation: Marco (A) used his math background, and Victor (C) used his pilot's discipline.

questions.question: describes a specific physical difficulty that was harder than expected?
questions.explanation: Marco (A) mentions neck and shoulder discomfort, and Victor (C) mentions lung capacity.

questions.question: admits to having had prior concerns or having an unexpected emotional response?
questions.explanation: Sarah (B) felt an emotional release, and Victor (C) worried about his age.

questions.question: reflects on a broader philosophical lesson learned about the art or the process?
questions.explanation: Marco (A) realized music isn't about perfection, and Victor (C) saw it as a form of meditation.

questions.question: highlights the value of the social support or community they found?
questions.explanation: Sarah (B) mentions the jazz community, and Elena (D) the camaraderie at band camp.

questions.question: describes a period of frustration or adjustment to the social or technical aspects?
questions.explanation: Victor (C) adjusted to the intrusive attention, and Elena (D) found the technical jargon confusing.

questions.question: acknowledges the specific external trigger that led them to start learning?
questions.explanation: Sarah (B) mentions her husband's gift, and Elena (D) mentions a viral video.
